commands.review_summary:
The branch modifies `src/commands/role.ts` to add a `/role maintainer-guest` subcommand with an ephemeral acknowledgment prompt before granting role `1503268035908075590`, restricted to users with role `1509063061598769333`.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new role-management feature rather than a reported current-main bug. The changed flow can be verified in Discord by exercising add, cancel or timeout, and removal paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer approval and contributor runtime proof rather than an automated repair because the remaining blocker is the privileged access policy and live Discord behavior evidence.

target.title: feat: add maintainer-guest role with acknowledgment prompt
target.body:
## What

Adds `/role maintainer-guest` (role ID `1503268035908075590`) as a new subcommand on the existing `/role` command.

## How it works

- **Adding the role:** Shows an ephemeral confirmation prompt warning that Maintainer Guest grants access to privileged Fake Slack Connect channels, and asks the runner to acknowledge that the person being added is aware of this. Only proceeds after clicking **"I understand"**. Clicking **"Cancel"** or letting it time out (5 min) does nothing.
- **Removing the role:** Skips the prompt entirely — just removes it, same as the other role toggles.

The acknowledgment pattern is pulled from the existing FSC `/maintainer fsc add-user` flow — same style of Container + buttons, same timeout handling.

## New classes

- `AcknowledgeButton` — dumb button for `replyAndWaitForComponent` (same pattern as `PromptButton` in maintainer.ts)
- `RoleToggleWithAck` — extends the role toggle concept with a required acknowledgment step before adding

Both are internal to `role.ts` and not exported.
